policy.title: RBI releases final guidelines on Climate Risk and Sustainable Finance for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI mandates all SCBs to integrate climate risk into ICAAP by March 2027, requiring physical risk stress testing across loan portfolios with geographic granularity. Banks must report climate-adjusted PD estimates for agriculture, real estate, and MSME sectors quarterly starting Q1 FY27.

policy.title: SEBI extends BRSR Core mandatory assurance to top 500 listed companies
policy.description: SEBI circular extends mandatory reasonable assurance on BRSR Core indicators to top 500 companies by market cap from FY27, up from top 150. Supply chain ESG disclosures now required for top 250 companies, including Scope 3 estimates.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI issues exposure draft on Parametric Climate Insurance Products for Agriculture and SMEs
policy.description: Draft guidelines permit parametric triggers based on IMD weather station data, satellite indices, and approved third-party hazard models. Insurers may partner with banks for bundled credit-insurance products with automatic payout mechanisms tied to rainfall deficit or flood depth thresholds.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: BCBS publishes Pillar 3 climate risk disclosure framework with phase-in timelines
policy.description: Basel Committee finalizes standardized climate disclosure templates for banks, requiring quantitative metrics on financed emissions and physical risk exposures by 2028. Jurisdictions may accelerate adoption; RBI signaled alignment in concurrent statement.

policy.title: ISSB releases guidance on IFRS S2 implementation for emerging market jurisdictions
policy.description: New guidance provides transition reliefs for Scope 3 reporting in emerging markets through 2028 and introduces proportionality principles for SME value chains. India's MCA signals formal adoption roadmap expected by Q3 2026.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk variables for South Asia
policy.description: Updated scenarios include granular physical hazard pathways for India covering compound flooding, agricultural heat stress, and cyclone intensification. RBI confirmed adoption of NGFS Phase V as reference for upcoming supervisory stress tests.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk analytics pilot
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a pilot with HDFC Bank to provide asset-level flood and cyclone risk scores for the bank's commercial real estate portfolio across coastal metros. Pilot covers 15,000 assets with risk scoring at 90m resolution.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory raises $8M Series A to expand natural capital analytics for Indian banks
competitors.description: Funding led by Omnivore and British International Investment will support GIST's expansion of biodiversity and water risk scoring for agricultural lending portfolios. Company claims coverage of 400+ districts with nature-related risk metrics.

competitors.title: Moody's launches Climate Risk Score for Indian MSME lending
competitors.description: New product provides physical and transition risk scores for unlisted MSMEs using GST, MCA, and satellite data proxies. Initial coverage targets 2 million enterprises; pricing positioned for integration into bank LOS systems.

competitors.title: XDI releases APAC Bank Climate Risk Benchmark covering 50 Indian financial institutions
competitors.description: Benchmark ranks Indian banks by portfolio exposure to physical climate hazards, finding 23% of assessed assets face high risk from flooding by 2050. Report publicly names top 10 most exposed lenders, generating media coverage.

competitors.title: Sprih partners with Yes Bank for BRSR compliance automation
competitors.description: Partnership will automate ESG data collection from Yes Bank's corporate borrowers for BRSR reporting and supply chain due diligence. Platform integration targets 500 mid-market borrowers in first phase.

competitors.title: First Street raises $75M Series C to expand into commercial lending and emerging markets
competitors.description: Round led by TPG Rise Climate will fund First Street's expansion beyond US residential into global commercial real estate and emerging market pilots. Company confirmed India feasibility study underway with unnamed banking partner.

research.title: Climate Risk in Indian Banking: Physical Hazards and Credit Portfolio Implications
research.description: Report estimates 12-18% of Indian bank agricultural loan books face elevated physical risk from compound heat-drought events by 2035. Recommends integration of climate-adjusted PD overlays into ECL models, citing RBI guideline alignment.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Lessons from Agricultural and SME Pilots
research.description: IFC study of 14 parametric insurance pilots in India and Bangladesh finds basis risk remains key adoption barrier, with 35% of payouts mismatched to actual losses. Recommends hybrid index designs and bundling with credit products to improve uptake.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Financial Sector Due Diligence
research.description: Analysis of ESG data availability across 40 emerging markets finds India ranks 8th globally but faces significant gaps in Scope 3, biodiversity, and social metrics for unlisted companies. Calls for alternative data approaches and regulatory safe harbors.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Financial Institutions: Methodological Advances
research.description: Working paper reviews 28 bank climate stress tests globally, finding wide variance in physical risk modelling approaches. Recommends standardization of hazard-exposure-vulnerability frameworks and forward-looking scenario calibration aligned with NGFS pathways.

research.title: Global Parametric Insurance Market: Growth Drivers and Emerging Structures
research.description: Swiss Re projects global parametric premium volume to reach $18B by 2030, with Asia-Pacific growing fastest at 24% CAGR. India identified as key growth market due to protection gap of $30B+ in climate-vulnerable agriculture and infrastructure.

research.title: Climate Finance Flows to India 2024-25
research.description: Tracked climate finance in India reached $68B in FY25, up 28% YoY, but adaptation finance remains under 10% of total. Report identifies green lending by commercial banks as fastest-growing channel, constrained by lack of standardized risk assessment tools.

ourRead.headline: RBI's March guidelines create immediate demand for climate-adjusted credit risk tools — StepChange must move fast before global players like Jupiter and Moody's capture Indian bank mandates.
body: The convergence of signals this month is unmistakable: Indian regulators have moved from consultation to mandate, global competitors are entering with serious capital, and the research consensus points to massive unmet demand for climate-credit integration. The window for first-mover advantage in Indian banking is narrowing but still open.

RBI's final climate risk guidelines (policy-1) are the most significant development. The March 2027 deadline for ICAAP integration with quarterly PD reporting creates urgent, budget-backed demand from every scheduled commercial bank. Critically, RBI's requirement for geographic granularity in physical risk stress testing plays directly to StepChange's hazard data capabilities. The simultaneous NGFS Phase V release (policy-6) with enhanced South Asia variables gives us the scenario architecture banks need. Our PD/LGD modelling must be positioned as RBI-ready within 90 days.

Jupiter's HDFC pilot (comp-1) and Moody's MSME launch (comp-3) signal that global players view India as a priority market. However, both are entering with US-centric models being adapted for India — Jupiter's 90m resolution is coarse for Indian urban contexts, and Moody's MSME product relies on proxy data that will struggle with ground truth validation. XDI's public benchmark (comp-4) naming exposed banks creates both threat and opportunity: banks will seek defensible risk assessments, not just rankings. StepChange's advantage lies in India-native hazard data and local regulatory fluency — we must articulate this clearly against global competitors pitching 'emerging market expansions.'

The IFC parametric insurance study (research-2) delivers a critical insight: 35% payout mismatch is driving basis risk concerns that block adoption. This directly informs our parametric insurance bet. IRDAI's exposure draft (policy-3) permitting third-party hazard models creates a regulatory pathway, but only if we can demonstrate lower basis risk through better trigger design. StepChange should prioritize hybrid index development using our proprietary data and seek to be named as an approved hazard model provider in IRDAI's final guidelines.

Watch: First Street's India feasibility study (comp-6) — if they announce a banking partnership, competitive dynamics shift significantly. Action: Schedule presentations with 10 largest SCBs in April, leading with RBI guideline alignment and March 2027 compliance timeline. Position StepChange as the only India-native climate risk platform that can deliver regulatory-ready PD overlays without the adaptation lag of global entrants.
